title: Nipah virus real-time RT-PCR (NiV-TM2018)
description: {"blocks":[{"key":"5vctl","text":"The protocol aims to specifically amplify Nipah viruses (NiV) and not other viruses.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"auvq8","text":"This is a modified version of a published assay. ","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"79tuj","text":"Modifications were to account for mismatches underneath oligo target sites.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}},{"key":"1410o","text":"The assay targets the nucleoprotein (N) gene region and is designed as a qualitative test for investigating NiV infection of humans.","type":"unstyled","depth":0,"inlineStyleRanges":[],"entityRanges":[],"data":{}}],"entityMap":{}}

steps: %s %s MODIFICATIONS TO THE PUBLISHED ASSAY: Forward primer is unmodified from that described in the original paper Reverse primer has a degeneracy added to the published version Probe has a degenercay added and the 3' terminal T removed, compared to the published version Conditions, concentrations and reagents used all differ from those originally published Addition of in vitro transcribed synthetic template oligonucleotide controls (PRIMER and PROBE controls) THE DESIGN PHILOSOPHY: to ensure our assay detects old and new variants of NiV
SuperScript‚ĄĘ III Platinum‚ĄĘ One-Step qRT-PCR Kit Life Technologies Catalog #11732088

The assay has been used on a Rotor-Gene 6000 real-time thermocycler Prepare sufficient mix for the number of reactions. Include a suitable 'dead volume' as necessary if using a robotic dispenser. %s %s MIX PREPARATION Reagent Volume (¬Ķl) x1 Final reaction concentration Nuclease-free water 4.35 N/A RRV E2 F 200pmol/¬Ķl 0.09 900nM RRV E2 R3 200pmol/¬Ķl 0.09 900nM BFV-FAM 100pmol/¬Ķl 0.03 150nM 2X Reaction Mix1 10 1X SuperScript¬ģ III/Platinum¬ģ Taq Mix1 0.4 1X ROX Reference Dye (25¬ĶM) 0.04 0.05¬ĶM Template 5 N/A TOTAL 20 Dispense 15¬ĶL to each reaction well. Add 5¬ĶL of template (extracted RNA, controls or NTC [nuclease-free water] ). Total reaction volume is 20¬ĶL

The definition used for a satisfactory positive result from a real-time fluorogenic PCR should include each of the following: A sigmoidal curve ‚Äď the trace travels horizontally, curves upward, continues in an exponential rise and followed by a curve towards a horizontal plateau phase A suitable level of fluorescence intensity as measured in comparison to a positive control (y-axis) A defined threshold (C T ) value which the fluorescent curve has clearly exceeded (Fig.1 arrow), which sits early in the log-linear phase and is <40 cycles A flat or non-sigmoidal curve or a curve that crosses the threshold with a C T >40 cycles is considered a negative result. NTCs should not produce a curve a Figure 1 . Examples of satisfactory sigmoidal amplification curve shape when considering an assay‚Äôs fluorescent signal output. The crossing point or threshold cycle (C T ) is indicated (yellow arrow); it is the value at which fluorescence levels surpass a predefined (usually set during validation, or arbitrary) threshold level as shown in this normalized linear scale depiction. LP-log-linear phase of signal generated during the exponential part of the PCR amplification; TP-a slowing of the amplification and accompanying fluorescence signal marks the transition phase; PP-the plateau phase is reached when there is little or no increase in fluorescent signal despite continued cycling.
creator: Ian M Mackay
